# Ingestion Checklist: Incremental Ingestion with Watermark and Manifest Tracking

**Purpose**: Verify correct implementation of the manifest-backed incremental ingestion layer — covering DDL, hash computation, two-phase commit, full-refresh, skip logic, watermark, backward compatibility, tests, and metadata/lineage.
**Created**: 2026-02-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

---

## Section 1: Manifest Table

- [ ] CHK001 `file_manifest` table created with correct DDL — all 8 columns: `file_path VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY`, `file_size_bytes BIGINT NOT NULL`, `file_hash VARCHAR NOT NULL`, `file_mtime DOUBLE NOT NULL`, `run_id VARCHAR NOT NULL`, `processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL`, `status VARCHAR NOT NULL`, `error_message VARCHAR`
- [ ] CHK002 `status` column has `CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'success', 'failed'))` constraint
- [ ] CHK003 `manifest_metadata` table created with columns: `source_dir VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY`, `watermark_mtime DOUBLE NOT NULL`, `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL`
- [ ] CHK004 Both tables use `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` (idempotent, safe to call on every run)
- [ ] CHK005 Migration guard present: `ALTER TABLE ingestion_runs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` for all four new columns (`files_checked`, `files_skipped`, `files_ingested`, `files_failed`)
- [ ] CHK006 `file_path` is the sole primary key — one row per file path (current-state table, not append-only log per Clarification Q4)

---

## Section 2: Hash Computation

- [ ] CHK007 `compute_file_hash()` opens the file in binary mode (`"rb"`) and streams raw bytes — no Parquet parsing occurs before or during hash computation (Clarification Q1)
- [ ] CHK008 SHA-256 algorithm used via `hashlib.sha256()`
- [ ] CHK009 Chunk size is 64 KB (`chunk_size: int = 65536`) for memory-efficient streaming
- [ ] CHK010 Uses sentinel-based iterator pattern: `iter(lambda: f.read(chunk_size), b"")`
- [ ] CHK011 Returns lowercase hex digest string (`h.hexdigest()`)
- [ ] CHK012 Function signature: `compute_file_hash(path: Path, chunk_size: int = 65536) -> str`

---

## Section 3: Two-Phase Commit

- [ ] CHK013 `upsert_pending()` is called **before** `_process_file()` for each candidate file (phase 1 of two-phase commit, FR-012)
- [ ] CHK014 `upsert_pending()` uses INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE to handle both first-time and repeat files atomically (Clarification Q5)
- [ ] CHK015 `upsert_pending()` sets `status = 'pending'`, `run_id = <current_run_id>`, and clears `error_message = NULL`
- [ ] CHK016 `mark_success()` is called **after** successful file ingestion (phase 2 — success path)
- [ ] CHK017 `mark_failed()` is called with a non-empty error message string on any exception during file processing (phase 2 — failure path)
- [ ] CHK018 `mark_failed()` is invoked inside an exception handler that does not re-raise silently — the pipeline continues processing remaining files after a single file failure
- [ ] CHK019 Crash recovery: files left in `status = 'pending'` state (from a prior crashed run) are treated as non-skippable and retried on the next run (FR-005 / SC-006)

---

## Section 4: Full Refresh

- [ ] CHK020 `--full-refresh` flag added to `ingest_transactions.py` argument parser with `action="store_true"` and `default=False`
- [ ] CHK021 `--full-refresh` flag is passed through to `run_pipeline(full_refresh=args.full_refresh)`
- [ ] CHK022 `run_pipeline` signature extended with `full_refresh: bool = False` keyword argument
- [ ] CHK023 `reset_all_to_pending(run_id)` is called at the start of the run (before file loop) when `full_refresh=True`
- [ ] CHK024 `reset_all_to_pending()` issues a bulk `UPDATE file_manifest SET status = 'pending', run_id = ?, error_message = NULL` — does NOT delete and reinsert rows (Clarification Q3)
- [ ] CHK025 Watermark is reset to `NULL` (or `manifest_metadata` row is deleted/zeroed) on `--full-refresh` to force re-evaluation of all file mtimes
- [ ] CHK026 Full refresh reprocesses ALL files in `source_dir` regardless of their current manifest state
- [ ] CHK027 Files with no existing manifest entry during full refresh are handled identically to first-time ingestion (INSERT with `pending`)

---

## Section 5: Skip Logic

- [ ] CHK028 `should_skip(file_path, file_hash)` returns `True` **only** when a manifest entry exists with `status = 'success'` AND `file_hash` matches the stored hash (FR-003)
- [ ] CHK029 `should_skip()` returns `False` for files with `status = 'failed'` — failed files are always retried (FR-005)
- [ ] CHK030 `should_skip()` returns `False` for files with `status = 'pending'` — pending entries from prior crashes are retried (SC-006)
- [ ] CHK031 `should_skip()` returns `False` when the hash differs from the stored hash, even if `status = 'success'` — content change triggers re-ingestion (FR-004)
- [ ] CHK032 `should_skip()` returns `False` (and `get_manifest_entry()` returns `None`) for files never seen before — new files always processed
- [ ] CHK033 Skip check is performed after hash computation — `compute_file_hash()` is called for all non-watermark-filtered files before `should_skip()` is consulted

---

## Section 6: Watermark

- [ ] CHK034 `get_watermark(source_dir)` queries `manifest_metadata` by `source_dir` and returns `None` safely when no row exists (first run or after full-refresh reset)
- [ ] CHK035 Watermark is used as a pre-filter: files with `file.stat().st_mtime <= watermark` are skipped without calling `compute_file_hash()` (FR-010)
- [ ] CHK036 Watermark pre-filter is bypassed when `full_refresh=True`
- [ ] CHK037 `update_watermark()` is called after the file processing loop with the maximum `st_mtime` across all **successfully ingested** files in the current run
- [ ] CHK038 `update_watermark()` uses INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE to upsert the watermark row for the `source_dir`
- [ ] CHK039 Watermark is read from DuckDB at pipeline startup (not from in-memory state), ensuring correctness across process restarts (US5 scenario 3)
- [ ] CHK040 On `--full-refresh`, watermark in `manifest_metadata` is reset so all files are candidates for the mtime pre-filter check

---

## Section 7: Backward Compatibility

- [ ] CHK041 All existing Feature 002 test files pass without modification after this feature is merged (SC-008, FR-013)
- [ ] CHK042 `validator.py`, `dedup.py`, and `loader.py` are not modified (plan.md — "UNCHANGED")
- [ ] CHK043 Default `run_pipeline()` call (no `full_refresh` argument) behaves identically to pre-feature behavior for the first run — all files are discovered and processed
- [ ] CHK044 `ingest_transactions.py` invoked without `--full-refresh` triggers incremental mode; no behavioral change for existing callers
- [ ] CHK045 Existing DuckDB databases without the manifest tables are handled transparently: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` and `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` ensure no errors on upgrade
- [ ] CHK046 `run_pipeline()` remains callable programmatically (not only via CLI) with the same return type (`RunResult`) — existing callers are not broken

---

## Section 8: Tests

- [ ] CHK047 `test_compute_file_hash_deterministic` — same file path produces the same hash on two calls
- [ ] CHK048 `test_compute_file_hash_detects_byte_change` — modifying one byte of a file changes the returned hash
- [ ] CHK049 `test_manifest_store_create_tables_idempotent` — calling `create_tables()` twice raises no error
- [ ] CHK050 `test_upsert_pending_insert_and_update` — inserting a new entry succeeds; upserting the same path updates the row in place
- [ ] CHK051 `test_should_skip_true_when_success_and_same_hash` — returns `True` for success + matching hash
- [ ] CHK052 `test_should_skip_false_when_failed` — returns `False` when status is `failed` (FR-005)
- [ ] CHK053 `test_should_skip_false_when_hash_changed` — returns `False` when hash differs even if status is `success` (FR-004)
- [ ] CHK054 `test_mark_success` — status updated to `success`, `processed_at` refreshed
- [ ] CHK055 `test_mark_failed` — status updated to `failed`, `error_message` populated
- [ ] CHK056 `test_watermark_get_set` — `get_watermark()` returns `None` before any write; returns stored value after `update_watermark()`
- [ ] CHK057 `test_reset_all_to_pending` — bulk update sets all rows to `status = 'pending'` and clears `error_message`
- [ ] CHK058 `test_full_refresh_flag_reset_behavior` — `--full-refresh` invocation calls `reset_all_to_pending()` and processes all files regardless of prior manifest state
- [ ] CHK059 Integration test `test_second_run_skips_already_processed` — second run with no new files reports 0 files ingested and N files skipped (US1 scenario 1)
- [ ] CHK060 Integration test `test_new_file_only_processed` — only the new file is ingested when one new file is added after initial run (US1 scenario 2)
- [ ] CHK061 Integration test `test_modified_file_detected_and_reingested` — file replaced in-place with different content is re-ingested; manifest entry updated with new hash and run_id (US2 scenario 1, SC-005)
- [ ] CHK062 Integration test `test_full_refresh_processes_all_files` — all files processed after `--full-refresh` regardless of manifest state (US3, SC-004)
- [ ] CHK063 Integration test `test_run_id_links_manifest_to_ingestion_runs` — every `file_manifest.run_id` joins to a valid `ingestion_runs` row with zero orphans (SC-003)

---

## Section 9: Metadata & Lineage

- [ ] CHK064 `RunResult` dataclass extended with `files_checked: int = 0`, `files_skipped: int = 0`, `files_ingested: int = 0`, `files_failed: int = 0`, `full_refresh: bool = False` (FR-014, plan.md models.py section)
- [ ] CHK065 `RunResult.summary()` output includes "Mode: Incremental" or "Mode: Full refresh — manifest bypassed" line (SC-007)
- [ ] CHK066 `RunResult.summary()` includes files checked, skipped, ingested, and failed counts
- [ ] CHK067 `ingestion_runs` table extended with `files_checked`, `files_skipped`, `files_ingested`, `files_failed` INTEGER columns (DEFAULT 0)
- [ ] CHK068 `complete_run()` in `loader.py` accepts and persists all four new manifest-stat keyword arguments
- [ ] CHK069 `file_manifest.run_id` is set to the current `run_id` at `upsert_pending()` time (phase 1) so crash recovery preserves the correct run linkage (Clarification Q5)
- [ ] CHK070 `file_manifest` is queryable directly via DuckDB SQL without any additional tooling (FR-016)

---

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline when an item reveals a deviation from spec
- Items are numbered CHK001–CHK070 for easy cross-reference in code review and PR comments
- Constitution alignment: Principles I (quality gate via manifest), II (lineage via run_id FK), IV (DuckDB queryable table), V (current-state schema, graceful migration)
